titleStmt.title: Letter from Mrs MacFeely to Ellen Daly, 23 April 1923
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Mrs MacFeely

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Daisyhill
Ballybofey
Co Donegal 23.4.'23 Dear Mrs Daly Every so many
thanks for your letter.
We all know too well that
your dear son died a noble
death as he was such a great
favourite with everybody. I
was told that his guardsmen
in Drumboe shed tears when
poor young Daly was been
led out. As he was spoken
of as a true Patriot of Ireland
Poor boy he is gone to enjoy
the reward of a well spent life
It is the dear mother, father,
brothers and sisters tha he
has left behind to mourn
but trust you will be resigned
go God's Holy Will + you will all
be united in Heaven where
so sorrow will be no more. I
would be so thankful for
his photo + his last letter
that we may ever treasure
We have had masses said
for the four brave boys +
we never orget them in
our prayers although they do
not want them. When my daughter was at
home from Dublin for Easter
she told me your dear daughter
was ill + she had not heard
the sad news. She felt so much
for her. Miss O'Donnell + Miss Blake
are in Buncrana still + my
niece M O'Flaherty is home
from Mountjoy although she is not able
to stop at home as they live
on the border. As soon as you find it
convenient we will be waiting
on our dear friend's photo
for Memoriam Cards. And again
accepting our sincerest sympathy Yours very sincerely,
Mrs. MacFeely
